name: stitch-ui-design
description: Expert guide for creating effective prompts for Google Stitch AI UI design tool. Use when user wants to design UI/UX in Stitch, create app interfaces, generate mobile/web designs, or needs help crafting Stitch prompts. Covers prompt structure, specificity techniques, iteration strategies, and design-to-code workflows for Stitch by Google.
capabilities: Guide users in crafting effective prompts for Google Stitch AI UI generator
Provide best practices for specifying UI components, layouts, and visual styles
Offer example prompts for various design scenarios (dashboards, e-commerce, mobile apps)
Explain how to create multi-screen app flows with iterative refinement
Show how to specify responsive behavior and platform-specific designs
Teach users how to export designs to HTML/CSS, Figma, and code
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown files with guidance for Google Stitch AI UI design tool. All static analyzer findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting, not shell execution; cryptographic algorithm flags are documentation text misinterpreted by scanner. No executable code, network requests, or dangerous patterns present.
valueStatement: This skill helps users craft effective prompts for Google Stitch AI UI design tool. It provides best practices, example prompts, and advanced techniques to generate professional web and mobile interfaces.
userTitle: Create UI Designs with Google Stitch
limitations: Skill only provides prompting guidance - it does not generate designs directly
Does not have access to Google Stitch API or tool execution capabilities
Cannot verify or test generated designs - only offers prompting advice
Google Stitch is an experimental tool with evolving capabilities

useCases.targetUser: Design beginners, developers trying AI design tools
useCases.title: Learn Stitch Prompting Basics
useCases.description: New users wanting to understand how to write effective prompts for Google Stitch to generate their first UI designs

useCases.title: Improve Design Output Quality
useCases.description: Users who have tried Stitch but get generic results and want to learn specific techniques to get better, more tailored designs

useCases.title: Create Multi-Screen App Flows
useCases.description: Users building complete applications who need guidance on prompting for multiple connected screens and responsive layouts

promptTemplates.title: Basic Single Screen Prompt
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a [component type] with [specific features], using [color scheme] theme and [layout style] design.
promptTemplates.scenario: Simple page or component design request

promptTemplates.title: E-commerce Product Page
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an e-commerce product page with hero image gallery, add-to-cart button, reviews section, and related products carousel. Use clean minimalist design with [accent color] accents and generous white space.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a complete product page with multiple sections

promptTemplates.title: Mobile App Screen
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a [screen name] screen for a [app type] mobile app. Include [list of features and components]. Use [design style] with [color palette]. Add [interactive states] and [navigation elements].
promptTemplates.scenario: Mobile app screen with specific UI components

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Screen App Flow
promptTemplates.prompt:
Create a [app type] app with:
- [screen 1] with [features]
- [screen 2] with [features]
- [screen 3] with [features]
Use [design system] style and ensure responsive behavior across mobile and desktop.
promptTemplates.scenario: Complete multi-screen application with multiple pages

bestPractices: Always specify visual details: colors, typography, spacing, and design style to avoid generic AI outputs
List each screen explicitly for multi-screen apps - Stitch will confirm before generating multiple designs
Include functional requirements: button actions, form validation, navigation patterns, and user flows
antiPatterns: Using vague prompts like 'Create a nice login page' - results in generic, unusable designs
Forgetting to specify platform (mobile vs desktop) leading to wrong layout expectations
Not defining visual style, causing AI to output random or inconsistent aesthetics

faq.question: What is Google Stitch?
faq.answer: Google Stitch is an experimental AI UI generator powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash that transforms text prompts and visual references into functional UI designs for web and mobile applications.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Google Stitch?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides prompting guidance and best practices. It does not have access to execute Google Stitch or generate designs directly.

faq.question: What file formats can Stitch export to?
faq.answer: Google Stitch supports export to HTML/CSS, Figma, and code formats depending on the design created.

faq.question: Can I create responsive designs with Stitch?
faq.answer: Yes, specify responsive behavior in your prompts. Describe how the design should adapt from mobile (320px) to desktop (1440px) dimensions.

faq.question: How do I get better results from Stitch?
faq.answer: Be extremely specific in your prompts: list all components, specify colors and themes, describe layouts in detail, and include functional requirements like button actions and navigation.

faq.question: Is Google Stitch free to use?
faq.answer: Google Stitch is currently an experimental tool available through Google Labs. Usage terms may vary; check Google Labs for current availability and pricing.
